mentions.note: Still has premium multiple from growth, but unclear if capable of beating numbers anymore; package food business is 'new coal' in this market.
Merger talks with Unilever food business (Hellmann's, Coleman's, Knorr); combined company cuts costs and owns supermarket shelf space.

Unilever food business merger (Hellmann's, Colman's, Knorr) creates aisle dominance and synergy value. Trading 16x, 3.81% yield; currently unloved but deals prove strategic strength. Patient holders rewarded in 6 months.
Recommended over Campbell's; transformational Unilever Foods deal creating scale, flavor positioning, cost synergies despite dilution and debt risks.

CEO Brendan Foley interview: Cramer gave free pass on giant Unilever acquisition (Hellman's mayo/brands); didn't press on cost despite skepticism; in retrospect should have asked tougher questions about execution vs. Unilever.
Hit new lows; market hasn't caught into benefits of McCormick's acquisition of Helmets business from Unilever; sentiment remains sour on entire food group.
Stock crushed; Wall Street thinks Unilever food business acquisition is colossal overpay. Waiting to hear management's justification at earnings.
